title: SSOT Registry — Declaration dependency graph (interop issue #13)

dep.$comment: A directed dependency edge: `from` directly uses `to` (the arrow points at the prerequisite). Endpoints reference the greppable declaration handle (effective-current `qualified`), resolved by query — NOT the surrogate id. This is a DERIVED, zero-curation collection: whole-collection REPLACE on re-extract.

id.$comment: Opaque sjv-minted surrogate — DETERMINISTIC uuid5 of the (from,to,kind) triple (not random uuid4), because deps are derived + REPLACE-on-re-extract + referenced by nothing, so a stable content-derived id makes a re-extract byte-identical (clean diffs, true no-op). Identity is the (from,to,kind) triple; deduped on import.

from.$comment: The DEPENDENT declaration's effective-current qualified name (its type or proof references `to`). downstream(X)=dependents={from : edge(to=X)}.

to.$comment: The DEPENDENCY / prerequisite declaration's effective-current qualified name. upstream(X)=dependencies={to : edge(from=X)}.

kind.$comment: Optional enrichment: 'type' = `to` appears in `from`'s signature (hard structural dep, constrains module boundary); 'proof' = only in the proof term (softer); null = unclassified. The extractor MAY populate it.

$comment: id uniqueness is enforced in consumers/deps/rules.py. Cross-collection reference integrity (from/to must resolve to an effective-current declaration qualified) is enforced at the store level (consumers/store.py::deps_reference_integrity) — it reuses the Issue 5 Part 2 reconcile match-key. Acyclicity is NOT enforced (Lean 'mutual' blocks are legitimate); view('deps','cycles') is a convenience projection only.
